title: Qualitative Capstone — qualitative analysis of analyst response to AI-introduced corruption
public_claim: The submitted qualitative capstone on whether structured analyst review can catch AI-introduced evidentiary corruption.
distinctive_mechanism: It uses fifty matched-pair scenarios, deliberately corrupted evidence packets, Structured Analytic Technique execution, sealed coding lanes, human decision review, triad reconciliation, APEX review rounds, P0/P1/P2 severity closure, G1-G5 checks, per-scenario re-coding notes, and all-fifty claim-level human validation to keep the claim bounded.
use_value: Its value is not that it proves AI safety generally; it shows a disciplined way to study when human analysts function as a meaningful check on AI-assisted analytic error, while stating that the fifty-two-field re-coding lane, runtime MAS factorial, and full Blue Cell execution remain deferred rather than falsely completed.

minimum_public_demo: Test one representative case; confirm input (packet name, source files, version signal, scope...) produces output (what the packet is and what decision it can still...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.
